components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Used for main calls to action.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 11px 22px; border-radius: 8px; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 600; transition: all 0.2s ease; cursor: pointer; background: #3b82f6; color: #fff; border: none; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #2563eb; transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(59, 130, 246, 0.25); }

components.name: Data Stat Box
components.css: .ds-stat-box { font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif; background: #121212; border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.07); border-radius: 12px; padding: 22px; text-align: center; } .ds-stat-num { font-family: 'DM Sans', sans-serif; font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 800; color: #3b82f6; display: block; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-stat-label { font-size: 0.8rem; color: #888888; }

narrative.overview: The FinGate design system is built to present complex biometric security concepts with absolute clarity. Drawing heavy inspiration from Apple's technical web presentations, the interface relies on deep dark mode contrasts, structured whitespace, and precise typography to guide the reader through system architectures and evaluation data. It is unapologetically technical, prioritizing substance over decoration.
keyCharacteristics: High-contrast technicality: Deep blacks against crisp whites.
Structured whitespace: Generous padding around components to isolate concepts.
Typographic hierarchy: Distinct separation between structural headers and readable data.

rules.name: The Focus Rule
rules.body: The primary blue accent is strictly reserved for interactive elements, active steps in workflows, and crucial statistics. Never use it as a decorative background or large fill.

rules.name: The Data Clarity Rule
rules.body: Text must never overflow or overlap. Always prioritize the readability of technical specs and tables over decorative sizing.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only as a response to state (e.g., a subtle glow on the primary button hover) and never as structural scaffolding.

dos: Do use structured whitespace to separate different technical modules (SWOT, Data, Roadmap).
Do rely on the hairline border (rgba(255,255,255,0.07)) to define card edges instead of relying on background contrast alone.
Do ensure text contrast stays high; use #f0f0f0 for core reading material.
donts: Don't use playful, colorful, or highly decorative visual styles.
Don't use heavy parallax animations or slow, distracting transitions. Keep motion minimal and functional.
Don't use dense, overlapping layouts. Give the data room to breathe.
Don't use glassmorphism or heavy drop shadows as a default card style.
